receiver_rule: Trial-wave outcomes are bounded self-improvement results. They may inform future patches or parking decisions, but they do not become governance truth, identity truth, or hot-memory authority.

candidate_record.baseline_story: Consumer shells historically risked drifting on short-board meaning, closeout reading, surface-versioning summaries, and fallback order.
candidate_record.candidate_story: The bounded consumer contract, surface versioning, and drift-validation wave now keep the same first-hop story across Codex-style, Claude-style, dashboard, and observer consumers.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: shared interpretation fracture while one shell still looks cleaner locally
candidate_record.rollback_path: revert packaging/readout changes and fall back to Tier-1 session-start ordering
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: aligned packaging is not improved reasoning or governance quality
candidate_record.scope_limit: packaging and shared interpretation only; no reasoning-runtime or identity change

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Consumer-parity packaging now reads as aligned across the bounded shells that matter most.
unresolved_items: alignment is packaging-level only, not proof of better reasoning
future consumer changes must keep running the same drift wave
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize council, identity, or transport-semantics changes
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: cross-consumer alignment is not outcome calibration or self-improvement of cognition
analyzer_closeout.next_action: reuse this drift-validation wave whenever shared consumer packaging changes

result_surface.carry_forward_rule: may inform future consumer-packaging trials, but does not authorize runtime, governance, or identity promotion

candidate_record.baseline_story: Operator retrieval had boundaries and landing-zone ideas, but no final query contract and no safe carry-forward posture for later operator use.
candidate_record.candidate_story: The operator-retrieval contract now defines query classes, provenance-first return shape, and non-promotion rules so retrieval can stay auxiliary.
candidate_record.success_metric: operator_retrieval_contract_present and compiled_landing_zone_spec_present and live runner maturity
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: retrieval reading as runtime truth before a real compiled corpus and safe runner exist
candidate_record.rollback_path: revert retrieval-facing packaging and keep retrieval in planning-only posture
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: a query contract is not a live retrieval system or solved memory layer
candidate_record.scope_limit: operator-facing retrieval packaging only; no runtime truth or identity promotion

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Operator retrieval packaging is now bounded and safer to reason about, but it is not live enough to promote as an active capability.
analyzer_closeout.evidence_bundle_summary: operator retrieval contract present; compiled landing-zone spec present; no live retrieval runner
unresolved_items: no live operator-retrieval runner is in the mainline yet
no compiled corpus or collection health lane exists yet
no operator validation wave has tested retrieval against real use
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize retrieval answers to outrank session-start, observer-window, packet, or identity surfaces
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: operator-retrieval packaging is not solved knowledge memory and not runtime authority
analyzer_closeout.next_action: revisit only after a live bounded retrieval runner and compiled collections exist

result_surface.carry_forward_rule: may guide future retrieval packaging, but does not authorize retrieval answers to outrank runtime truth

candidate_record.baseline_story: Deliberation-mode hinting already pushed bounded feature work toward lightweight review, but shells could still over-read escalation ladders as active current pressure.
candidate_record.candidate_story: The deliberation-mode hint now separates `active_escalation_signals`, `conditional_escalation_triggers`, and `review_cues` so later agents can keep a lightweight path lightweight without hiding real escalation pressure.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: lightweight paths still look heavier than they are, or shells drift on escalation meaning
candidate_record.rollback_path: revert to the prior deliberation-hint packaging and keep the admitted candidate as history only
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: better deliberation hinting is not better deliberation quality or better reasoning
candidate_record.scope_limit: session-start packaging only; no council-runtime, claim-truth, or identity change

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Deliberation-mode hint packaging now cleanly separates active escalation pressure from conditional escalation ladders.
analyzer_closeout.evidence_bundle_summary: deliberation_hint_probe mode=lightweight_review active=0 conditional=2 review=5 split=yes
unresolved_items: packaging wins do not prove better reasoning quality
future shell consumers must preserve the active/conditional distinction
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize council runtime depth changes, confidence math changes, or broader shell expansion
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: better escalation packaging is not better council independence, accuracy, or calibration
analyzer_closeout.next_action: keep this split stable while evaluating one next admitted self-improvement candidate

result_surface.carry_forward_rule: may inform future routing-packaging trials, but does not authorize deeper council by default

candidate_record.baseline_story: Task-board parking helpers existed, but later agents could still read parking guidance as soft prose rather than a routing outcome.

candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: better parking clarity is not better planning quality or better governance
candidate_record.scope_limit: task-board routing packaging only; no task.md authority rewrite or auto-promotion

analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: better parking clarity is not better planning, authority, or human approval

candidate_record.baseline_story: Shared-edit preflight already guarded blocked, coordinate, claim-first, and clear outcomes, but shells could still blur path collision with self-claim gaps into one generic caution.
candidate_record.candidate_story: Shared-edit preflight now surfaces decision basis, explicit other-overlap paths, claim-gap paths, and bounded pressure flags so later agents can tell coordination friction from claim discipline at a glance.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: clearer overlap packaging turns into a fake permission system or hides the need to coordinate first
candidate_record.rollback_path: restore the prior shared-edit preflight packaging and keep the result as history only

candidate_record.scope_limit: shared-edit packaging only; no new permission layer, no transport change, no governance semantics rewrite

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Shared-edit preflight now shows overlap pressure and claim-gap pressure as separate bounded signals.

unresolved_items: clearer shared-edit packaging does not prove better collaboration outcomes

analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize automatic claims, path locks, broader write permissions, or governance changes

candidate_record.baseline_story: Mutation preflight already exposed decision points, but next_followup still pointed too statically and could lag behind the current bounded friction.
candidate_record.candidate_story: Mutation preflight now routes next_followup to the current narrowest bounded hook so successors can move toward shared-edit, publish/push, or task-board review without stale defaults.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: dynamic follow-up routing behaves like a hidden planner or diverges across consumers

candidate_record.scope_limit: mutation-followup packaging only; no new hook family, no planner, no permission-system expansion

candidate_record.baseline_story: Surface versioning already exposed versions and parent lineage, but it still lacked one explicit compatibility posture showing which shells are repo-native entries, which are bounded adapters, and what fallback chain preserves parity.
candidate_record.candidate_story: Surface versioning now exposes compatibility posture so later agents can recover consumer roles and shared fallback order without guessing.
candidate_record.failure_mode_watch: compatibility packaging starts sounding like stronger interop truth or reopens transport mythology
candidate_record.rollback_path: remove compatibility posture and keep the prior versioning readout plus drift-wave history only
candidate_record.overclaim_to_avoid: clearer lineage packaging is not stronger vendor interop, shared cognition, or transport promotion
candidate_record.scope_limit: surface-versioning and consumer-lineage packaging only; no transport, hook, or governance mutation

analyzer_closeout.result_story: Surface versioning now exposes compatibility posture and fallback lineage clearly enough for later shells to recover parent truth without guessing.
unresolved_items: compatibility posture improves lineage clarity, not actual cross-vendor transport
future shells must keep the fallback chain subordinate to parent runtime truth
analyzer_closeout.promotion_limit: does not authorize new transport semantics, shell promotion, or vendor-native interoperability claims
analyzer_closeout.overclaim_warning: clearer versioning lineage is not stronger shared understanding or transport truth
analyzer_closeout.next_action: keep compatibility posture bounded while admitting the next candidate
